[[commons:Category:Hokura-jinja]][[ja:šŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ]] {{Expand Japanese|šŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hokura Shrine | native_name = šŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ | image = śė•„ĀģšŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hokura Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.735434|135.278061}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|ō≥Ŕąō≥ōßŔÜŔą|azb|ō≥Ŕąō≥ōßŔÜŔą|bcl|Susanoo|be|–°—É—Ā–į–Ĺ–į–į|bg|–°—É—Ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–ĺ|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|ő£őŅŌÖŌÉő¨őĹoőŅ|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|ō≥Ŕąō≥ōßŔÜŔą (ōģōĮōß)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|’ć’ł÷ā’Ĺ’°’∂’ł|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|„āĻ„āĶ„Éé„ā™|ka|ŠÉ°ŠÉ£ŠÉ°ŠÉźŠÉúŠÉĚŠÉĚ-ŠÉúŠÉĚ-ŠÉõŠÉėŠÉôŠÉĚŠÉĘŠÉĚ|ko|žä§žā¨ŽÖłžė§|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ÍĮĀÍĮ®ÍĮĀÍĮÖÍĮ®-ÍĮÖÍĮ£-ÍĮÉÍĮ§ÍĮÄÍĮ£ÍĮáÍĮ£|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|–°—É—Ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–ĺ|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|–°—É—Ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–ĺ|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|ŗģöŗĮĀŗģöŗģĺŗģ©ŗģĶŗĮč|th|ŗłčŗłĻŗłčŗł≤ŗĻāŗłôŗĻāŗł≠ŗłį|tr|Susanoo|uk|–°—É—Ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–ĺ|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|Áī†śąĒŚóöŚįä|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hokura Shrine'''|šŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki JinmyŇćchŇć]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|ō¨ŔäŔÜō¨ōß|arz|ō∂ōĪŔäō≠ ōīŔÜō™Ŕą|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Ň†intoistick√° svatynńõ|da|Shinto-tempel|de|ShintŇć-Schrein|el|ő£őĻőĹŌĄőŅŌäŌÉŌĄőĻőļŌĆ őôőĶŌĀŌĆ|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ňúintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|ŔÖōĻō®ōĮ ōīŘĆŔÜō™ŔąŘĆŘĆ|fi|Ň†intolainen pyh√§kk√∂|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|ŗ§∂ŗ§Ņŗ§āŗ§§ŗ•č ŗ§ģŗ§āŗ§¶ŗ§Ņŗ§į|hr|Ň†intoistińćki hram|hu|Sint√≥ szent√©ly|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|Á•ěÁ§ĺ|ka|ŠÉęŠÉėŠÉúŠÉęŠÉėŠÉź|ko|žč†žā¨ (žč†ŪÜ†)|lb|ShintŇć-Schr√§in|mr|ŗ§∂ŗ§Ņŗ§āŗ§§ŗ•č ŗ§ģŗ§āŗ§¶ŗ§Ņŗ§į|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ŗ¨úŗ¨Ņŗ¨ěŗ≠ćŗ¨úŗ¨ĺ|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xinto√≠sta|ru|–Ē–∑–ł–Ĺ–ī–∑—Ź|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Ň†intoistińćno svetiŇ°ńće|sr|–®–ł–Ĺ—ā–ĺ–ł—Ā—ā–ł—á–ļ–ł —Ö—Ä–į–ľ|sv|Jinja|th|ŗł®ŗł≤ŗł•ŗĻÄŗłąŗĻČŗł≤ŗłäŗłīŗłôŗĻāŗłē|tr|Ňěinto tapńĪnańüńĪ|uk|–°–≤—Ź—ā–ł–Ľ–ł—Č–Ķ (—Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–ĺ)|vi|ThŠļßn Kobe|it|Higashinada-ku|ja|śĚĪÁĀėŚĆļ|ko|ŪěąÍįÄžčúŽāėŽč§ÍĶ¨|ru|–•–ł–≥–į—Ā–ł–Ĺ–į–ī–į-–ļ—É (–ö–ĺ–Ī–Ķ)|th|ŗĻÄŗłāŗłēŗłģŗłīŗłáŗł≤ŗłäŗłīŗłôŗł≤ŗłĒŗłį|tr|Higashinada|vi|Higashinada, location (P625) == * {{coord|34.735434|135.278061}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * śė•„ĀģšŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * * {{ill|Q3028663|ltq=Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|1=Toshigami}} * {{ill|Q11120574|ltq=Q11120574|qid=Q11120574|1=Saonetsuhiko}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181073 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * „ĀĽ„ĀŹ„āČ„Āė„āď„Āė„āÉ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005000066 == Commons category (P373) == * Hokura-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Q3199052|ltq=Q3199052|qid=Q3199052|1=Koshikiiwa Shrine}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine|de|Nishinomiya-Schrein|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|es|Santuario Nishinomiya|fr|Nishinomiya-jinja|ja|Ť•ŅŚģģÁ•ěÁ§ĺ|ru|–•—Ä–į–ľ –Ě–ł—Ā–ł–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź|zh|Ť•ŅŚģģÁ•ěÁ§ĺ|qid=Q705297|1=Nishinomiya Shrine}} * {{ill|Q135039223|ltq=Q135039223|qid=Q135039223|1=Ohokuninushino Shrine}} * {{ill|Ohokuninushino Shrine|lt=Ohokuninushino Shrine|qid=Q135270424|1=Ohokuninushino Shrine (Q135270424)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': 'ŚÖĶŚļęÁúĆÁ•ěśąłŚłāśĚĪÁĀėŚĆļśú¨ŚĪĪÁĒļŚĆóÁēĎ680', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hokura-jinja}} [[JA:šŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ]] {{wikidata link|Q17218056}} == Japanese content == {{Á•ěÁ§ĺ | ŚźćÁßį=Hokura Shrine | ÁĒĽŚÉŹ=[[File:śė•„ĀģšŅĚšĻÖŤČĮÁ•ěÁ§ĺ.jpg|270px|Weeping cherry in the precinct]]
Haiden | śČÄŚú®Śúį={{ill|lt=HyŇćgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=HyŇćgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|qid=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}} Motoyama-chŇć Kitabatake 680 | šĹćÁĹģ={{Coord|34|44|07.9|N|135|16|41.0|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Hokura Shrine}} | Á•≠Á•ě={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ŇĆkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=ŇĆkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}
{{ill|lt=Toshigami|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=ŇĆtoshi-mioya-no-Mikoto|1=Toshigami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiine-tsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Á§ĺś†ľ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}ÔľąsmallÔľČ
former {{ill|lt=gŇćsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=GŇć-sha}} | ŚČĶŚĽļ=Unknown | śú¨śģŅ={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing | šĺčÁ•≠={{ill|lt=May 4|Q2549|qid=Q2549|1=May 4}}„ÉĽ{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|lt=5th|1=May 5}} | ŚúįŚõ≥ = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Hokura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Hokura Shrine''' („ĀĽ„ĀŹ„āČ„Āė„āď„Āė„āÉ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} on Mt. Hokura (mid-slope of {{ill|lt=KinchŇćzan|Q97658192|qid=Q97658192|1=KinchŇćzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Motoyama-chŇć (Kobe)|Q11520195|qid=Q11520195|lt=Motoyama-chŇć|1=MotoyamachŇć}}, {{ill|lt=Higashinada Ward|Q277434|qid=Q277434|1=Higashinada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=HyŇćgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=HyŇćgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gŇćsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=GŇć-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ŇĆkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=ŇĆkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}, {{ill|lt=Toshigami|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=ŇĆtoshi-mioya-no-Mikoto|1=Toshigami}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiine-tsuhiko|Q11120574|qid=Q11120574|lt=Shiine-tsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}}. ===History=== Details of the foundation are unknown, but many groups of {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} inside and outside the precinct suggest an ancient ritual site. Various relics from the Stone Age, Bronze Age, and late Yayoi period have been excavated from inside and outside the shrine grounds, including Yayoi pottery, stone axes, and stone swords; most famous is a c.20 cm bronze halberd (Important Cultural Property). All are considered ritual-use objects. ''Hafuri'' pottery and glass magatama were also found, indicating worship from very early times''Shrines of Kobe: Guide to 310 shrines in the city'', 2000, Kobe branch of HyŇćgo Prefecture Shrine Office.. According to the shrine history, the shrine name derives either from (1) descendants of Shiine-tsuhiko-no-Mikoto, such as Kurabito Mizumori, worshiping their ancestor, or (2) storing captured weapons from the campaign against the Three Han. The original main deity was Shiine-tsuhiko-no-Mikoto, who appeared at {{ill|lt=Hayasui-no-to|Q11639656|qid=Q11639656|1=Hayasui-no-to}} ({{ill|lt=Akashi Strait|Q845336|qid=Q845336|1=Akashi Strait}}) during {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu‚Äôs eastern expedition|Q11590444|qid=Q11590444|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}} and led the army''Historical Walks of HyŇćgo Prefecture, Upper Volume: Kobe/Hanshin/Awaji'', 2006, Yamakawa Shuppansha.. A legend says Shiine-tsuhiko arrived at ŇĆgi beach in {{ill|lt=ŇĆgi (Kobe)|Q11661589|qid=Q11661589|lt=ŇĆgi|1=ŇĆgi}}, south of Hokura Shrine, riding on the back of a blue turtle (Ňćgi), and that this became the origin of the place name ŇĆgi. Yoshii Yoshitaka inferred that this shrine was originally Oku-ebi-sha of Nishinomiya Ebisu, with Shiine-tsuhiko as chief of the maritime people controlling the north side of Osaka Bay''Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 3 Settsu/Kawachi/Izumi/Awaji'', 2000, ed. Tanigawa Kenichi et al., Hakusuisha.. The "Sumiyoshi Taisha shrine records" mention the name Fukurofu-yama, and ''Settsushi'' records possession of a ridge tag from a major repair in KenchŇć 2. The sacred fire at the shrine‚Äôs light platform is the eternal lamp called "Nada no hitotsubi." Tradition says that when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} lost his route at sea at night while returning from the {{ill|lt=Kumaso|Q1196897|qid=Q1196897|1=Kumaso}} expedition, he saw the light of Hokura Shrine and safely returned to Naniwa. Since then, as in the old song, "for boats offshore, a guide to rely on, the single light of Nada," it was widely revered as a maritime guide. ===Festivals=== * January 20 - ŇĆdawara Festival * May 4-5 - Annual grand festival * July 14 - Nagoshi Festival * August 11 - Ancestor-deity festival ===Around the shrine=== West of Hokura Shrine is Hokura Plum Grove, planted in 1975 (ShŇćwa 50), with about 150 white-plum trees and about 100 red-plum trees including Shirakaga and Maya-kŇćbai[https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/b07715/kuyakusho/higashinadaku/shoukai/shoukai/ume.html Kobe City website: Higashinada highlights - plum blossoms]. Also between this shrine and the plum grove runs a hiking trail from {{ill|lt=KinchŇćzan|Q97658192|qid=Q97658192|1=KinchŇćzan}} via {{ill|lt=Kaze-fuki-iwa|Q97674252|qid=Q97674252|1=Kaze-fuki-iwa}} to the highest peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Rokko|Q462120|qid=Q462120|1=Mount RokkŇć}}. Because it is close to the urban area, like {{ill|lt=Futatabi-yama|Q11394928|qid=Q11394928|1=Mount Futatabi}}, {{ill|lt=Takatori-yama|Q11668943|qid=Q11668943|1=Takatori mountain}}, and Hatafuri-yama, ‚Äúdaily climbing‚ÄĚ is popular and many climbers have exceeded 1,000 ascents. On the southern {{ill|lt=fault scarp|Q475080|qid=Q475080|1=Fault scarp}} where the zigzag approach path runs up to the shrine, slope countermeasure works commissioned by the {{ill|lt=Rokko Sabo Office|Q18457447|qid=Q18457447|1=Rokko Sabo Office}} have been conducted since 2016, including frame and anchor works to reduce {{ill|lt=slope failure|Q3574985|qid=Q3574985|lt=collapse on steep slopes|1=slide}}. ===Access=== *{{ill|lt=Hankyu Railway|Q473058|qid=Q473058|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Kobe Main Line|Q1054520|qid=Q1054520|lt=Kobe Line|1=HankyŇę KŇćbe Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Okamoto Station (HyŇćgo)|Q3970335|qid=Q3970335|lt=Okamoto Station|1=Okamoto Station (HyŇćgo)}}, 1 km northeast *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kobe Line|Q613302|qid=Q613302|lt=TŇćkaidŇć Line|1=JR KŇćbe Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Motoyama Station|Q1042797|qid=Q1042797|1=Settsu-Motoyama Station}}, 1.3 km northeast === Notes === {{ŤĄöś≥®„Éė„Éę„Éó}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat-inline|hokura-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hokura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hokura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai ShŇćsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:śĚĪÁĀėŚĆļ„Āģś≠īŚŹ≤]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:śú¨ŚĪĪ (Á•ěśąłŚłā)]]
